page: Amazon Web Services m8ine.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized virtual machine instance. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features dedicated Intel x86_64 processors running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The hardware configuration delivers 48 virtual CPUs derived from 24 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, paired with 192.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be fulfilled via network-attached Elastic Block Store volumes. To support high-throughput storage and network operations, the m8ine.12xlarge provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. It does not feature any hardware GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8ine.12xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise-scale workloads that require high network throughput and dedicated compute resources without the overhead of local storage. This configuration is optimized for general-purpose applications, web serving, database servers relying on EBS, containerized microservices, and continuous integration pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services m8ine.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features dedicated Intel x86_64 processors operating at 3.9 GHz, providing 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of memory, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. To support demanding data transfers, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. This resource profile makes the m8ine.12xlarge highly suitable for enterprise applications, web serving, containerized environments, and database workloads that require high network throughput and dedicated compute resources without local storage requirements.
